zh.lifeSummary: 當Elisabeth Dirk Wouter van Beugen Beughem Boeghen Bogen Beughem Boeghen Bogen於大約1315年出生在荷蘭努-布拉班特時，她的父親Dirk Wouter Jan van Boechem26歲，母親Elisabeth Arnoud van Moenle van HINTHAM26歲。她與Heimerik Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1399年之後。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elisabeth Dirk Wouter van Beugen Beughem Boeghen Bogen Beughem Boeghen Bogen于大约1315年出生在荷兰努-布拉班特时，她的父亲Dirk Wouter Jan van Boechem26岁，母亲Elisabeth Arnoud van Moenle van HINTHAM26岁。她与Heimerik Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1399年之后。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Beugen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German and Jewish (Ashkenazic): habitational name from any of various places called Bergen.
Dutch and Flemish: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill (see   Berg  ), or a habitational name from any of various places so named (see   Van Bergen  ).
Swedish (mainly Bergén): ornamental or habitational name formed with the (placename) element berg ‘mountain, hill’ + the adjectival suffix -en/-én, a derivative of Latin -enius ‘relating to’.

variants.name: Berg
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lennart, Bjorn, Lars, Nils, Alf, Nels, Thor, Tor, Knute, Selmer, Anders.

German and Dutch: topographic name for someone who lived on or by a hill or mountain, from Middle High German berc, Middle Dutch berghe. This surname is also found in some other parts of Europe, e.g. in France (Alsace and Lorraine) and Poland. Compare Dutch Vandenberg .
Norwegian and Danish: habitational name from a farmstead named with Old Norse bjarg ‘mountain, hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Boeghen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh and English (Norfolk): probably also a variant of   Baughan  , which is itself the base-form from which   Vaughan   originates.

English (Norfolk): apparently a variant of Bowgen, a nickname from Old French bouzon, bougon ‘large arrow, crossbow bolt (as a heraldic device)’, perhaps used metaphorically of a person who bore some physical resemblance to a bolt. Compare Busson 1.

variants.name: Boughan
meanings: English (Durham): variant of   Boughen   and   Baughan  .
Irish: from Ó Buadhacháin, see Bohan .

variants.name: Busson
meanings: English (Gloucestershire; of Norman origin): nickname from Old French bouzon, buzun ‘large arrow, crossbow bolt (as a heraldic device)’, perhaps used metaphorically of a person who bore some physical resemblance to a bolt. Compare Boughen 1.
English (Gloucestershire): perhaps a variant of   Bushen  .
French: metonymic occupational name for a cooper, from a derivative of Old French busse ‘barrel’.

variants.name: Baughan
meanings: Welsh: from the adjective bychan ‘little’, a diminutive of bach (see Bach 4), originally in general used to distinguish father and son bearing the same name in genealogies. This word usually appears in Anglicized surnames in the lenited form Vaughan .
History: A Welsh family of this name was established from an early date in Great Rollright, Oxfordshire, England. The name was probably brought to North America from Oxfordshire rather than Wales.

variants.name: Buffin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Gaile, Yvon.
English and Welsh: variant of   Boughen  .
French: from a diminutive of Old French bufe, buffe ‘blow, slap in the face’, hence probably a nickname for a rough or uncouth man.

nameMeanings.name: Bogen
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Otto, Fritz, Kurt.
German: metonymic occupational name for an archer, bowman, or bow maker, from Middle High German boge ‘bow’.
Jewish (Ashkenazic): artificial name from German (Regen)bogen ‘rainbow’ or from German Bogen (Yiddish boigen) ‘arch, bow’.

variants.name: Boge
meanings: German: from the personal name Boio (possibly meaning ‘boy’; see Boy 1 and Boye 1); or a variant of Bogen .
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farms in southwestern Norway named Boge, from Old Norse bugr ‘hook, bay, bend’, with reference to an inlet or a bend in a river.

variants.name: Bogin
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Jewish Asher, Rimma, Sinai.
Jewish:
(from Belarus): habitational name from a village called Bogin.

nameMeanings.name: Elisabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

variants.name: Elizabeth
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

nameMeanings.name: Dirk
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Flemish and Dutch form of Derek . Its use in the English-speaking world since the 1960s is largely due to the fame of the actor Dirk Bogarde ( 1921–99 , born Derek Niven van den Bogaerde ). He was of Dutch descent, although he was actually born in Scotland. The manly image of the name has been reinforced by its coincidence in form with Scottish dirk ‘dagger’ (from Gaelic durc).

nameMeanings.name: Wouter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: form of   Walther .

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Elisabeth Dirk Wouter van Beugen Beughem Boeghen Bogen Beughem Boeghen Bogen was born about 1315, in North Brabant, Netherlands, her father, Dirk Wouter Jan van Boechem, was 26 and her mother, Elisabeth Arnoud van Moenle van HINTHAM, was 26. She married Heimerik Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 4 daughters. She died after 1399.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elisabeth Dirk Wouter van Beugen Beughem Boeghen Bogen Beughem Boeghen Bogen est née environ 1315, à Brabant-septentrional, Pays-Bas, son père, Dirk Wouter Jan van Boechem, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elisabeth Arnoud van Moenle van HINTHAM, avait 26 ans. Elle a épousé Heimerik Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée après 1399.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elisabeth Dirk Wouter van Beugen Beughem Boeghen Bogen Beughem Boeghen Bogen um 1315 in Nord-Brabant, Niederlande geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Dirk Wouter Jan van Boechem 26 und ihre Mutter Elisabeth Arnoud van Moenle van HINTHAM 26. Sie hat Heimerik Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb nach 1399.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elisabeth Dirk Wouter van Beugen Beughem Boeghen Bogen Beughem Boeghen Bogen è nata il circa 1315 nel Brabante settentrionale, Paesi Bassi, sua padre, Dirk Wouter Jan van Boechem, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Elisabeth Arnoud van Moenle van HINTHAM, aveva 26 anni. Lei sposato Heimerik Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 4 figlie. Morì dopo il 1399.

ja.lifeSummary: Elisabeth Dirk Wouter van Beugen Beughem Boeghen Bogen Beughem Boeghen Bogenが約1315年にオランダノオルド-ブラバントで生まれた時，父Dirk Wouter Jan van Boechemは26歳，母Elisabeth Arnoud van Moenle van HINTHAMは26歳であった。Heimerik Jan Heimerik van Dordrechtと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1399年以後に死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elisabeth Dirk Wouter van Beugen Beughem Boeghen Bogen Beughem Boeghen Bogen(이)가 대략 1315년 네덜란드 노르드-브라반트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Dirk Wouter Jan van Boechem(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elisabeth Arnoud van Moenle van HINTHAM(은)는 26세였다. 그녀는 Heimerik Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1399년 이후 사망했다.

Ela casou-se com Heimerik Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu após 1399.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elisabeth Dirk Wouter van Beugen Beughem Boeghen Bogen Beughem Boeghen Bogen родилась около 1315 г. в местности нурд-брабант, Нидерланды, её отцу по имени Dirk Wouter Jan van Boechem было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elisabeth Arnoud van Moenle van HINTHAM, 26. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Heimerik Jan Heimerik van Dordrecht. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 4 дочери. Она умерла после 1399 г.
